title: They Love Crypto. They’re Trying to Buy the Constitution.
content: How a “financial flash mob” is trying to raise $20 million for a rare copy of the U.S. Constitution.

title: Visions of a U.S. Computer Chip Boom Have Cities Hustling
content: Many local governments see a silver lining in the shortage of semiconductor chips that has contributed to a slowdown in the global economy.
en_title: Visions of a U.S. Computer Chip Boom Have Cities Hustling
en_content: Many local governments see a silver lining in the shortage of semiconductor chips that has contributed to a slowdown in the global economy.

title: What Apple’s New Repair Program Means for You (and Your iPhone)
content: Apple said it would soon provide parts, tools and manuals to those who wanted to fix their own iPhones and Mac computers.
en_title: What Apple’s New Repair Program Means for You (and Your iPhone)
en_content: Apple said it would soon provide parts, tools and manuals to those who wanted to fix their own iPhones and Mac computers.

title: Investors Hung Their Hats on Peloton and Zoom Last Year. What Now?
content: Some “stay-at-home” stocks that were pandemic-era darlings have experienced brutal sell-offs.
en_title: Investors Hung Their Hats on Peloton and Zoom Last Year. What Now?
en_content: Some “stay-at-home” stocks that were pandemic-era darlings have experienced brutal sell-offs.

title: How Americans’ Appetite for Leather in Luxury SUVs Worsens Amazon Deforestation
content: An examination of Brazil’s immense tannery industry shows how hides from illegally deforested ranches can easily reach the global marketplace. In the United States, much of the demand for Brazilian leather comes from automakers.

more_info.byline: By Manuela Andreoni, Hiroko Tabuchi, Albert Sun and Victor Moriyama

en_title: How Americans’ Appetite for Leather in Luxury SUVs Worsens Amazon Deforestation
en_content: An examination of Brazil’s immense tannery industry shows how hides from illegally deforested ranches can easily reach the global marketplace. In the United States, much of the demand for Brazilian leather comes from automakers.

title: The Tech Gifts That Are Hard to Buy This Holiday Season
content: The list of scarce gadgets is long, driven by a chip shortage. Don’t wait until Black Friday for the best deals.
en_title: The Tech Gifts That Are Hard to Buy This Holiday Season
en_content: The list of scarce gadgets is long, driven by a chip shortage. Don’t wait until Black Friday for the best deals.

title: Staples Center in Los Angeles will be renamed Crypto.com Arena.
content: The rebranding, which will take effect on Christmas, is the latest example of a technology boom company’s paying to rename a well-known sports arena.
en_title: Staples Center in Los Angeles will be renamed Crypto.com Arena.
en_content: The rebranding, which will take effect on Christmas, is the latest example of a technology boom company’s paying to rename a well-known sports arena.

title: The Worst of Both Worlds: Zooming From the Office
content: Work life for many is in a mushy middle ground, and what’s at stake isn’t just who is getting talked over in meetings. It’s whether flexibility is sustainable, even with all the benefits it confers.
en_title: The Worst of Both Worlds: Zooming From the Office
en_content: Work life for many is in a mushy middle ground, and what’s at stake isn’t just who is getting talked over in meetings. It’s whether flexibility is sustainable, even with all the benefits it confers.

title: Developers Friendly With the Mayor Meet Their Match: Upper East Siders
content: The New York Blood Center wants to build a 233-foot-tall tower on a residential block in the Manhattan neighborhood. The city has proposed $450 million in tax incentives.

title: Women’s Tennis Challenges China’s Narrative Over Missing Player

title: Miramax Sues Quentin Tarantino Over Planned ‘Pulp Fiction’ NFTs
content: The director announced this month that he was selling nonfungible tokens based on the screenplay for his 1994 movie. Miramax asserted in court documents it still has “broad rights” to the film.
en_title: Miramax Sues Quentin Tarantino Over Planned ‘Pulp Fiction’ NFTs
en_content: The director announced this month that he was selling nonfungible tokens based on the screenplay for his 1994 movie. Miramax asserted in court documents it still has “broad rights” to the film.

title: Amazon, railing against high fees, will stop accepting Visa credit cards issued in Britain.
content: For years merchants have complained about transaction fees that eat into their revenue, but having Amazon take up the fight may ratchet up the pressure. The change will go into effect Jan. 19.

more_info.byline: By Eshe Nelson and Adam Satariano

en_title: Amazon, railing against high fees, will stop accepting Visa credit cards issued in Britain.
en_content: For years merchants have complained about transaction fees that eat into their revenue, but having Amazon take up the fight may ratchet up the pressure. The change will go into effect Jan. 19.

title: Travel Industry Takes Crucial First Step Toward Combating Climate Change

content: He was a groundbreaking master of the visual pun, part of a generation of designers for whom the aesthetic was in service to the idea.
